game_name: Street Fighter II: The World Warrior

sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation found in sources

special_mechanics.name: Special Command Moves
special_mechanics.description: Command-based special moves executed through joystick and button combinations. Game features multiple unique special moves per character.

special_mechanics.name: Combo System
special_mechanics.description: Hit combo system allowing consecutive hits to chain together for increased damage.

special_mechanics.name: Grabbing and Throwing
special_mechanics.description: Close-range grabbing and throwing mechanics available as basic moves.

special_mechanics.name: Blocking
special_mechanics.description: Defensive blocking mechanic accessible via joystick input.

special_mechanics.name: Crouch
special_mechanics.description: Crouching stance available through joystick input, affects hitboxes and enables low attacks.

tier_notes: Tier 1 data is qualitative/empirical. Sources provide extensive game design documentation and character profiles but contain no quantified physics parameters (velocities, acceleration values, frame data, or sub-pixel measurements). Street Fighter II is a fighting game rather than platformer; traditional platformer physics parameters (gravity, jump height, fall speed) are not applicable. Combat mechanics focus on command inputs, positioning, and attack/defense systems rather than character movement physics.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Street Fighter II: The World Warrior (SNES)
